examples.en: They will throw in a gratuitous box of chocolates if you spend $30 or more.
examples.zh: 消費滿 30 元，店家就會贈送一盒免費的巧克力。

examples.en: The film was criticized for its gratuitous violence.
examples.zh: 這部電影因為不必要的暴力場面而遭人批評。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Eating good chocolate gives me a sense of intense gratification.
examples.zh: 吃高級的巧克力讓我感到非常滿足。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Those “friends” are a bunch of selfish ingrates.
examples.zh: 那些所謂的「朋友」，都是一些自私自利、忘恩負義的傢伙。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He ingratiates himself with his boss by being helpful.
examples.zh: 他處處幫忙，為了討老闆歡心。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Success brings with it the danger of complacency.
examples.zh: 成功帶來自滿的危險。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 樂於取悅他人的，殷勤的

examples.en: If someone asked him for an autograph, he was complaisant enough to oblige.
examples.zh: 有人請他簽名，他都會欣然同意。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 不能安撫的，無法平息的

examples.en: He has an implacable hatred for his political opponents.
examples.zh: 他對政治對手的仇恨無法化解。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The angry customer was not placated by the clerk’s apology.
examples.zh: 即使店員道歉，憤怒的客人依舊無法消氣。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The control group only had vitamin pills as a placebo.
examples.zh: 對照組只拿到作為安慰劑的維生素片。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The placid skies belie an impending storm.
examples.zh: 平靜的天空，掩蓋了風暴將至的事實。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: She was secretly enamored of the prince.
examples.zh: 她偷偷迷戀著王子。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The company’s website looks amateurish.
examples.zh: 公司的網站看起來不是很專業。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The contract negotiations were reasonably amicable.
examples.zh: 合約談判算是相當和平。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The movie was assailed by critics.
examples.zh: 電影遭到影評的攻擊。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Patrons of the bar were having a desultory discussion about the news of the day.
examples.zh: 酒吧客人就當天的新聞隨意地閒聊。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The crowd cheered in exultation.
examples.zh: 群眾興奮地歡呼。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The man demonstrated an indomitable resilience in the face of misfortune.
examples.zh: 此人面對不幸，表現出不屈不撓的韌性。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 突出的，顯著的，最重要的

examples.en: She began to summarize the salient points of the proposal.
examples.zh: 她開始總結提案裡的要點。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Price wasn't a factor in my decision to buy the land.
examples.zh: 我決定買這塊地，價格並非考慮因素。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Extramarital affairs are a main cause of divorce.
examples.zh: 外遇是離婚的主因。

examples.en: Our annual conference is an important affair.
examples.zh: 我們的年會是件很重要的大事。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Artificial flowers seldom look natural.
examples.zh: 人造花很少看起來很自然。

examples.en: The robot has artificial intelligence built in.
examples.zh: 機器人內建有人工智慧。

examples.en: Many ancient artifacts were unearthed at the site.
examples.zh: 許多古文物在這個位址出土。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You must get a certificate before you can teach in this state.
examples.zh: 你得先取得證照才能在本州教書。

examples.en: The man is certified to teach in this state.
examples.zh: 此人通過檢定可以在本州教書。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: All the defects had been corrected before the new product was introduced to the market.
examples.zh: 新產品上市前已經修正了所有瑕疵。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

derivatives.meaning: [名] 效果，功效 [動] 做出，達到

examples.en: Both remedies are effective, but taking a shot is clearly more efficient.
examples.zh: 兩種治療方式同樣有效，但打針顯然效果更好。

examples.en: Taking an aspirin is an efficacious remedy for your headache.
examples.zh: 吃阿斯匹靈對你的頭痛很有效。

examples.en: The union accepted the terms, in effect capitulating to the management.
examples.zh: 工會接受了條件，等於向管理階層投降。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 簡單的，容易的；熟練的

examples.en: This problem requires more than just a facile solution.
examples.zh: 這個問題無法用簡單的方法解決。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He is a wonderfully facile writer.
examples.zh: 他是一名相當熟練的作家。

examples.en: The company is adding a ramp to facilitate the entry of wheelchairs.
examples.zh: 公司正添設坡道，方便輪椅進入。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He had great facility with words.
examples.zh: 他的文字能力甚強。

examples.en: A library is an important facility for research.
examples.zh: 圖書館是重要的研究設施。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 全體教師；能力；身體機能

examples.en: "On behalf of the faculty, welcome to our school," said the Principal to the visitors.
examples.zh: 「謹代表全體教師，歡迎光臨本校。」校長對訪客說。

examples.en: The old man is still in possession of all his faculties.
examples.zh: 老人的所有身體機能仍舊健全。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: We need to come up with a feasible plan to save the company.
examples.zh: 我們得提出可行的計畫來拯救公司。

examples.en: The proposal is theoretically feasible.
examples.zh: 這項提議理論上可行。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The manager suggested that we do a feasibility study before making a decision.
examples.zh: 經理建議我們進行可行性研究，然後再做決定。

examples.en: The CEO performed a remarkable feat by turning the company back into profitability.
examples.zh: 執行長達成重大成就，讓公司回歸獲利。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Don Quixote is a fictitious character.
examples.zh: 唐．吉訶德是個虛構的角色。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: My cell phone has several additional functions.
examples.zh: 我的手機有幾項附加功能。

examples.en: It also functions as a digital camera.
examples.zh: 它還可當作數位相機使用。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: His optimism infected everyone around him.
examples.zh: 他的樂觀精神感染到周遭的所有人。

examples.en: This computer is infected with a destructive virus.
examples.zh: 電腦遭到破壞性病毒感染。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Put some disinfectant on the wound to prevent infection.
examples.zh: 傷口擦點消炎藥以防止感染。

examples.en: This factory manufactures hi-tech instruments.
examples.zh: 這家工廠生產高科技儀器。

examples.en: The manufacture of clothing and shoes has largely relocated to areas where labor cost is low.
examples.zh: 成衣與鞋類的製造已經大量轉移到勞工成本低的地方。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The receptionist greeted every visitor with the same perfunctory smile.
examples.zh: 接待員以同樣敷衍的笑容，招呼每一位訪客。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The computer engineer is proficient in hunting viruses.
examples.zh: 這名電腦工程師擅長抓取病毒。

examples.en: This job requires proficiency in two languages.
examples.zh: 這份工作要求精通兩種語言。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 有機的；整體的；不可或缺的

examples.en: Organic chemicals are required in the manufacture of plastics.
examples.zh: 生產塑膠要用到有機化學品。

examples.en: Urban planners think of the city as an organic whole.
examples.zh: 都市規劃者把城市當作有機整體看待。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The police used coercion to obtain a confession from the suspect.
examples.zh: 警方用強制手段取得嫌犯的告白。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This ergonomic chair looks weird but is comfortable to sit on.
examples.zh: 這張人體工學椅造型怪異，但坐起來很舒服。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The two companies have found synergy since they merged.
examples.zh: 兩家公司合併後達到了相乘的效果。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 勞工；勞動 [動] 賣力，費力

examples.en: The car parts themselves are inexpensive; it’s the labor that will cost you.
examples.zh: 汽車零件本身不貴，貴的是工錢。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 精密的；複雜的 [動] 詳盡闡述；發揮

examples.en: They took elaborate precautions before going abroad.
examples.zh: 他們在出國之前採取了嚴密周到的預防措施。

examples.en: My thesis advisor asked me to elaborate more fully on the point I raised.
examples.zh: 我的論文指導教授叫我詳盡說明文中提出的重點。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Fetching water from the stream is a laborious task.
examples.zh: 到溪裡打水是很費力的工作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: We'll have to cooperate if we wish to succeed.
examples.zh: 要想成功，我們就得合作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 策略；行動 [動] 操控

examples.en: Through a series of legal maneuvers, the defense lawyer kept her client out of jail.
examples.zh: 透過一連串的法律行動，被告的律師成功讓她的當事人免於牢獄之災。

examples.en: The other directors are trying to maneuver her into resigning.
examples.zh: 其他董事正想方設法讓她辭職。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 作業；營運；（軍事）行動；開刀

examples.en: Desert Storm was a successful military operation.
examples.zh: 沙漠風暴是一項很成功的軍事行動。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He lives in an opulent mansion filled with priceless art and antiques.
examples.zh: 他住的豪宅，滿是無價的藝術品與骨董。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Stability and progress are two conflicting demands.
examples.zh: 穩定與成長是兩項有所抵觸的要求。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 經常的，恆定的 [名] 常數

examples.en: The thermostat on the air-conditioning keeps the room at a constant temperature.
examples.zh: 空調的溫度調節器讓室內溫度維持恆溫。

examples.en: There are two constants and one variable in this math formula.
examples.zh: 這道數學公式中有兩項常數、一項變數。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The Constitution guarantees individual rights.
examples.zh: 憲法保障個人權利。

examples.en: He has a strong constitution and seldom falls sick.
examples.zh: 他體質強健，很少生病。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Do you believe in the existence of ghosts?
examples.zh: 你信有鬼嗎？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.literal_meaning: not / together / be / 形容詞字尾

examples.en: What he does is inconsistent with what he says.
examples.zh: 他的言行不一。

examples.en: The witness's statements lack consistency.
examples.zh: 證人的證詞前後不一致。

examples.en: A separate chamber in the refrigerator keeps butter at the consistency you like.
examples.zh: 冰箱內有個獨立隔間可以讓奶油維持你喜歡的軟硬度。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 任命，使就職；架設，裝設

examples.en: The college recently installed its first woman president.
examples.zh: 大學第一位女校長最近上任。

examples.en: The computer comes with the software already installed.
examples.zh: 電腦送來時已經有內建軟體。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 分期付款；（書籍、影集等）一集

examples.en: You may pay for the car in 12 installments.
examples.zh: 這輛車你可以分12期付款。

examples.en: The latest TV miniseries will be presented in five installments.
examples.zh: 最新的電視迷你影集將分成五集播出。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 機構 [動] 設立，推行

examples.en: That think tank is a research institute in geopolitics.
examples.zh: 那所智庫是地緣政治的研究機構。

examples.en: By instituting these programs, we hope to improve our children’s education.
examples.zh: 藉由推行這些計畫，我們希望改善兒童教育。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The institution of these reforms is absolutely necessary.
examples.zh: 這些改革的推行絕對有必要。

examples.en: This is the most prestigious educational institution in the nation.
examples.zh: 這是全國最高學府。

examples.en: Do you think homosexuality threatens the institution of marriage?
examples.zh: 你認為同性戀會威脅到婚姻制度嗎？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: If the pain persists, you must see a doctor.
examples.zh: 如果疼痛一直持續，你得去看醫生。

examples.en: The boy is most persistent in his unreasonable demands.
examples.zh: 小男孩對於他的不合理要求非常堅持。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Mosquitoes breed in stagnant water.
examples.zh: 死水會滋生蚊子。

examples.en: The economy has remained stagnant for the third consecutive year.
examples.zh: 連續三年，經濟維持停滯。

examples.en: The government is pumping money into public projects to bring the country out of economic stagnation.
examples.zh: 政府挹注資金到公共工程，希望帶領國家走出經濟停滯。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: “Afraid of one’s own shadow” is a stale expression.
examples.zh: 「連自己的影子都怕」是個陳腔濫調的說法。

examples.en: The air in the deserted house is stale.
examples.zh: 廢棄房屋裡的空氣不新鮮。

examples.en: The leftover bread has grown stale.
examples.zh: 隔夜的麵包不新鮮了。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The player took up a stance, ready to hit the ball.
examples.zh: 球員採揮棒站姿準備打擊。

examples.en: Most students took an antiwar stance during the conflict.
examples.zh: 在這場衝突中，學生大多採取反戰立場。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 靜止的；靜電的 [名] 雜音

examples.en: Food prices remained static throughout the winter.
examples.zh: 糧食價格整個冬季維持平穩。

examples.en: Static electricity tends to be strong when humidity is low.
examples.zh: 濕度低，靜電會比較強。

examples.en: A good antenna can reduce the static on your radio.
examples.zh: 品質好的天線可以減少收音機的雜音。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The so-called fixed stars only appear to be stationary in the night sky.
examples.zh: 所謂恆星，只是在夜空中看起來靜止不動而已。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Statistics was developed from gambling.
examples.zh: 統計學是由賭博發展而來的。

examples.en: This statistic is incorrect, but all the other statistics are acceptable.
examples.zh: 這筆統計數字有誤，其他統計數據都可以接受。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 身高，高度；（身分、地位）崇高

examples.en: The boy reached a stature of six feet when he was 13.
examples.zh: 男孩13歲就長到六英尺高。

examples.en: He is a scientist of great stature.
examples.zh: 他是一名地位崇高的科學家。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Doctors and lawyers enjoy high social status.
examples.zh: 醫生與律師的社會地位很高。

examples.en: One’s car is often regarded as a status symbol.
examples.zh: 一個人所開的車，往往被視為身分地位的象徵。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The fishermen depended on cod for subsistence.
examples.zh: 漁民的生計全靠鱈魚。

examples.en: These villagers still pursue subsistence farming.
examples.zh: 這些村民仍然維持著自給式農業。

examples.en: The stray mountaineer subsisted on roots and insects for a week.
examples.zh: 迷路的登山客一週以來都靠吃草根與昆蟲為生。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Gold is a substance that defies decay.
examples.zh: 黃金是種不會腐朽的物質。

examples.en: There’s no substance to the malicious rumor.
examples.zh: 那則惡毒的謠言全然不實。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: There is a substantial amount of oil in these waters.
examples.zh: 這片水域底下有大量的石油蘊藏。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Many of the allegations have been substantiated by eye-witness testaments.
examples.zh: 許多指控已經獲得目擊者的證詞證實。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He resides in the capital with his family.
examples.zh: 他和家人住在首都。

examples.en: He is a permanent resident of the U.S., but not a citizen.
examples.zh: 他是美國永久居民，而非美國公民。

examples.en: Shops are not allowed in residential areas.
examples.zh: 住宅區禁止開店。

examples.en: That mansion is the mayor’s residence.
examples.zh: 那棟豪宅是市長官邸。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: My aunt is the most assiduous gardener I know.
examples.zh: 我阿姨是我所見過最勤快的園丁。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 異議人士 [形] 非主流的

examples.en: Political dissidents are tolerated in a democracy.
examples.zh: 民主社會會容忍政冶異議人士。

examples.en: The dissident writer keeps a column on the op-ed page.
examples.zh: 這名非主流作家在報紙專欄版中有自己的專欄。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The manager is presiding over a meeting.
examples.zh: 經理正在主持會議。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You should wash the vegetable thoroughly to get rid of any residues of pesticides.
examples.zh: 菜要洗乾淨，才不會有農藥殘留。

examples.en: The three daughters divided the residue of the estate among them.
examples.zh: 三個女兒均分剩餘的家產。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The complicated negotiations cannot be completed in one session only.
examples.zh: 複雜的談判，開一次會是解決不了的。

examples.en: Congress is in session now.
examples.zh: 國會正在會期中。

examples.en: The school team had a training session last weekend.
examples.zh: 校隊上週末集訓。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: After two days, flood waters began to subside.
examples.zh: 兩天後，淹水開始退了。

examples.en: The protests gradually subsided after the government made a few concessions.
examples.zh: 在政府做出幾項讓步之後，抗議逐漸平息下來。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 附屬機構，子公司 [形] 附屬的；次要的

examples.en: This company is a subsidiary of Microsoft.
examples.zh: 這家公司是微軟的附屬機構。

examples.en: Money was only a subsidiary factor in my decision to change jobs.
examples.zh: 我決定要換工作，而金錢只是其中的次要因素。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He is studying abroad under government subsidies.
examples.zh: 他在公費資助下出國留學。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The man wasn’t discouraged by the numerous challenges he encountered.
examples.zh: 此人遭遇許多挑戰，但他並沒有氣餒。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: There was complete concord among all the committee members, so the proposal was passed.
examples.zh: 全體委員看法一致，於是提案獲得通過。

examples.en: The party had long been plagued with discord, and finally split in two.
examples.zh: 這個政黨長久以來都有意見不一致的困擾，終於分裂為二。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The hostess gave cordial greetings to arriving guests.
examples.zh: 女主人熱情迎接到的賓客。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I believe the company should focus on its core expertise only.
examples.zh: 我認為公司應該只專注在核心專業上。

examples.en: What are the core values of this company?
examples.zh: 這家公司的核心價值為何？

examples.en: A lack of trust is at the core of the problem.
examples.zh: 問題的核心就在於不信任。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The war memorial was built to commemorate those killed in the war.
examples.zh: 建造了戰爭紀念碑來紀念戰亡者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The last hope for the prisoner lies in an amnesty from the governor.
examples.zh: 犯人的最後一線希望在於州長的特赦。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Dogs have been humans' companions from time immemorial.
examples.zh: 自古以來就是人類的同伴。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The retired President started writing his memoir.
examples.zh: 退休的總統開始撰寫回憶錄。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 紀念品（複數，無單數形）

examples.en: The boy is a Yankees fan and has collected a lot of baseball memorabilia.
examples.zh: 小男孩是洋基隊的球迷，收藏了許多棒球紀念品。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: After the meeting, the manager sent a memorandum to every participant.
examples.zh: 會議結束後，經理發一封備忘錄給所有與會者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Mnemonics—the use of mnemonic devices to facilitate memory—was invented by ancient Greeks.
examples.zh: 利用記憶手法來協助記憶，這種記憶術是古希臘人發明的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The old man likes to indulge in pleasant reminiscences of his childhood.
examples.zh: 老人喜歡沉浸於愉快的童年回憶中。

examples.en: I like this small town because it is reminiscent of my hometown.
examples.zh: 我喜歡這個小鎮，因為它令我想起我的故鄉。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Humility is a virtue.
examples.zh: 謙虛是美德。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The man’s a virtual dictator.
examples.zh: 此人形同獨裁者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He’s a real virtuoso in the kitchen.
examples.zh: 他在廚房裡是個不折不扣的大師。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The Army Corps of Engineers call themselves the ACE.
examples.zh: 陸軍工兵團自稱工牌部隊。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The man works for a multinational corporation.
examples.zh: 此人在一家跨國企業上班。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: There was a corpulent lady sitting next to me.
examples.zh: 有個胖女士坐在我旁邊。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This design incorporates the best features of our earlier models.
examples.zh: 這款設計融入了我們過去所有款式中的最佳特色。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: In the film, the house was haunted by an incorporeal entity.
examples.zh: 影片中的房子有個無形的東西在作祟。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The footage exposed the appalling carnage in that war-torn country.
examples.zh: 影片揭露出那個遭戰火摧殘的國家所發生的恐怖殺戮。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Lions are carnivores.
examples.zh: 獅子是肉食性動物。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

derivatives.meaning: [動] 化身；代表 [形] 化身的；典型的

examples.en: She is the very incarnation of grace.
examples.zh: 她就是優雅的化身。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The American brand of capitalism has numerous ills.
examples.zh: 美式資本主義有諸多弊病。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The teacher capitulated and allowed the use of calculators.
examples.zh: 老師屈服了，答應讓學生使用計算機。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The journalist was decapitated by terrorists.
examples.zh: 記者被恐怖分子斬首。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The movie recapitulates history truthfully.
examples.zh: 電影忠實地概括了歷史事件。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: We’ll do what we can to expedite the processing of your insurance claim.
examples.zh: 我們會盡力加快處理你的保險理賠。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 方便的，權宜的 [名] 權宜之計

examples.en: They found it expedient to negotiate with the terrorists.
examples.zh: 他們覺得和恐怖分子談判是比較容易的做法。

examples.en: The negotiation was regarded as a desperate expedient.
examples.zh: 談判被視為是已無可施的權宜之計。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The expedition reached the summit and returned without mishap.
examples.zh: 遠征隊抵達山頂後順利返回，沒有出事。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The country’s debt was a serious impediment to economic improvement.
examples.zh: 該國的債務對其經濟發展構成嚴重阻礙。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This is a residential area and peddlers are not allowed here.
examples.zh: 這裡住宅區，小販禁入。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 行人，徒步者 [形] 平淡的，缺乏想像力的

examples.en: There are no pedestrians on the sidewalk.
examples.zh: 人行道上不見行人。

examples.en: The pedestrian prose could really put me to sleep.
examples.zh: 平凡無奇的散文真是讓我睡覺。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The fire alarm caused a stampede to the exits.
examples.zh: 火警引發群眾往各個出口逃生。

examples.en: The frightened horses stampeded to the cliff and couldn’t stop.
examples.zh: 馬匹受到驚嚇，狂奔到懸崖邊卻停不下來。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I haven't seen him for so long that I probably can't recognize him.
examples.zh: 我好久沒見過他了，恐怕會認不出來。

examples.en: The 200-mile offshore fishing zone is internationally recognized.
examples.zh: 200英里的近海漁業區是國際公認的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: The explorer is fully cognizant of potential dangers.
examples.zh: 探險家十分清楚危險。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: The man is a cognoscente of medieval art.
examples.zh: 此人是中世紀藝術的專家。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: The character James Bond is portrayed as a connoisseur of fine wines.
examples.zh: 詹姆斯·龐德這個角色被刻畫為一名美酒鑑賞家。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: What's the doctor's diagnosis of your stomach ache?
examples.zh: 醫生對你肚子痛的診斷結果如何？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: The man ignored the "No Trespassing" sign and pressed ahead.
examples.zh: 此人忽視「禁止擅闖」的告示，繼續前進。

examples.en: I'm quite ignorant about computers.
examples.zh: 我對於電腦幾乎一無所知。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

words.definition: [形] 隱藏身分的 [副] 隱藏身分地

examples.en: The best-selling author remains incognito.
examples.zh: 這名暢銷作家的身分仍不為人知。

examples.en: The superstar likes to travel incognito.
examples.zh: 這位巨星喜歡匿名出遊。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

examples.en: The expert's prognosis of the new year is optimistic.
examples.zh: 專家對新的一年的展望相當樂觀。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「知道」與「相信」的字根

words.definition: [名] 信譽；信用；學分 [動] 相信

examples.en: Although she did the work, her supervisor took all the credit.
examples.zh: 雖然工作是她完成的，所有的功勞卻都被上司搶走。

examples.en: You can buy the car on credit, if you can afford the down payment.
examples.zh: 車子可以分期付款，只要付得起頭期款。

examples.en: This is a required, three-credit course.
examples.zh: 這是三學分的必修課程。

examples.en: Do you credit all his wild stories?
examples.zh: 你相信他那些匪夷所思的故事嗎？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 檢定合格的，獲得認可的

examples.en: This Medical School is accredited with the AMA and all major hospitals in the U.S.
examples.zh: 這家醫學院受到美國醫學會以及全美各大醫院的認可。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This candidate has excellent credentials for the kind of work we do.
examples.zh: 我們這一行來說，這名求職者具備極佳的資歷。

examples.en: You must show your credentials before you can enter.
examples.zh: 你得出示證件才可以進入。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This bank is our company's major creditor.
examples.zh: 這家銀行是我們公司主要的債權人。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Don't be so credulous.
examples.zh: 別太容易就相信人。

examples.en: There's an incredulous look on his face.
examples.zh: 他臉上一副狐疑的表情。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The Ten Commandments are important Christian creeds.
examples.zh: 十誡是重要的基督教信條。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: There is an incredible amount of coal in these mountains.
examples.zh: 這片山區的煤蘊藏量，數量多到令人難以置信。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I'm confident that I can win.
examples.zh: 我有自信會贏。

examples.en: My sister is a shy, diffident girl.
examples.zh: 我妹妹是個害羞、沒有自信的女孩。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You can confide in John completely.
examples.zh: 你可以完全信任約翰。

examples.en: May I confide something to you?
examples.zh: 我可以跟你透露一件事嗎？

examples.en: The information is confidential.
examples.zh: 這則資訊是機密。

examples.en: The CEO has made his confidant a manager.
examples.zh: 執行長指派他的親信出任經理。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The federal government operates on a huge budget.
examples.zh: 聯邦政府的運作需要一筆龐大的預算。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The most important principle in translation is "fidelity to the original."
examples.zh: 翻譯最重要的原則就是「忠於原文」。

examples.en: Sexual fidelity is essential to a happy marriage.
examples.zh: 性的忠誠是幸福婚姻的必要條件。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 異教徒；無宗教信仰者

examples.en: "Death to the infidels!" cried the fundamentalist.
examples.zh: 「異教徒去死！」基本教義派人士大聲疾呼。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [副] 不可信地，使人誤解地

examples.en: Some of Picasso's later drawings look deceptively simple.
examples.zh: 畢卡索晚期一些畫作看起來很簡單，但實際上並如此。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The auditorium was filled to capacity.
examples.zh: 觀眾席滿座。

examples.en: Both assembly lines are running at full capacity.
examples.zh: 兩條生產線都啟動，產能全開。

examples.en: That suitcase is truly capacious.
examples.zh: 那個手提箱是真正的大容量。

examples.en: I am capable of going two days without sleep.
examples.zh: 我能連續兩天不睡。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Drug capsules are sometimes hard to swallow, but they do make drugs less bitter.
examples.zh: 膠囊藥有時不易吞嚥，但確實讓藥不那麼苦。

examples.en: Every pupil was told to put together a time capsule to be buried somewhere on campus.
examples.zh: 每個小學生都被要求得組一個時間膠囊，在校園找個地方埋起來。

examples.en: The life capsule in a spacecraft is where astronauts live and work.
examples.zh: 太空船上的太空艙是太空人生活與工作的地方。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: When the war was over, the captives were released.
examples.zh: 戰爭結束後，俘虜獲得釋放。

examples.en: Captive animals sometimes demonstrate abnormal behavior.
examples.zh: 被囚禁的動物，有時會表現出異常的行為。

examples.en: Animals in captivity often live longer than those in the wild.
examples.zh: 圈養的動物，壽命往往比野生動物來得長。

examples.en: The superstar captivated the Shanghai audience.
examples.zh: 巨星征服了上海觀眾。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Small, imperceptible changes may accumulate to make a large difference.
examples.zh: 覺察不出的小改變可能會逐漸累積，最後產生重大的差別。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The company has been highly successful ever since its inception.
examples.zh: 這家公司從一開始就很有成功。

examples.en: A stuffy nose is one sign of an incipient cold.
examples.zh: 鼻塞是感冒開始的徵兆。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Army intelligence has intercepted an important piece of enemy communication.
examples.zh: 軍方情報單位攔截到一則重要的敵方通訊。

examples.en: The last-minute interception of the ball changed the result of the game.
examples.zh: 最後一秒抄球成功，改變了比賽結果。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The receptionist at the front desk told the visitor to wait a while.
examples.zh: 前檯的接待員請訪客稍候。

examples.en: I was a recipient of the Microsoft scholarship.
examples.zh: 我曾獲得微軟獎學金。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Minors, with their susceptible minds, should not watch this violent movie.
examples.zh: 未成年人的思想比較容易受到影響，不該看這部暴力片。

examples.en: The fees in this brochure are susceptible to change without further notice.
examples.zh: 手冊中提到的費用可能會會調整而不另行通知。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The contract preempts lawsuits by the company’s clients.
examples.zh: 合約中有條文可預防客戶對公司提出訴訟。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He is the current front-runner and thus the presumptive nominee.
examples.zh: 目前由他領先，因此以假定他會獲得提名。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The movie deals with a sinner’s search for redemption.
examples.zh: 這部電影的主題是罪人尋求救贖。

examples.en: The interview greatly aided the redemption of his reputation.
examples.zh: 這次面談對挽救他的名聲有幫助。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Normal services will be resumed in the spring.
examples.zh: 我們將於春季恢復正常的服務。

examples.en: Negotiations will resume after the long weekend.
examples.zh: 漫長的週末之後，談判將重新開始。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Games and team sports are subsumed under the classification of “recreation.”
examples.zh: 遊戲與團隊運動都涵蓋在「娛樂」這個分類底下。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 奢侈的，豪華的，豐盛的

examples.en: That was the most sumptuous feast I had ever enjoyed.
examples.zh: 那是我吃過最豐盛的一餐。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Check the contents of the wallet for clues about the owner.
examples.zh: 看看皮夾裡面的東西，找找有沒有失主的線索。

examples.en: I feel content with my current situation.
examples.zh: 我對現況感到滿意。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The police have the right to detain suspicious people for a short period of time.
examples.zh: 警察有權拘留可疑人等，但僅限短時間。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The maintenance of living standards is very difficult if prices keep rising like this.
examples.zh: 如果價格持續上漲，生活水平很難維持。

examples.en: I’m taking my car to the garage for maintenance.
examples.zh: 我要把車開到修車廠去保養。

examples.en: The suspect maintained that he was innocent.
examples.zh: 嫌犯堅稱自己無罪。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You have to obtain the teacher’s permission before you can leave early.
examples.zh: 你得先取得老師許可才能夠早退。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The company finds it hard to retain its market share under severe competition.
examples.zh: 面對激烈的競爭，公司發覺很難維持市占率。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The economy is expected to sustain its growth the whole year.
examples.zh: 全年的經濟預期將持續成長。

examples.en: The farmer grows only enough to sustain his family.
examples.zh: 農夫的作物只夠維持家人所需。

examples.en: The house sustained major damage in the earthquake.
examples.zh: 房子在地震中遭受重大損害。

examples.en: Sustainable development is the principle guiding the reclamation project.
examples.zh: 永續發展是這項開發計畫的指導原則。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: These tenacious farmers refuse to give up their traditional livelihood.
examples.zh: 這些頑強的農夫拒絕放棄他們傳統的生計。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The tenant is being evicted because he hasn’t been paying his rent.
examples.zh: 房客遭到驅逐，因為他一直不付房租。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: One basic tenet of modern science holds that any valid experiment must be duplicable.
examples.zh: 現代科學的一條基本信條：有效的實驗必須能夠複製。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: During his tenure, the Minister completed a number of major projects in public construction.
examples.zh: 部長在任內完成了幾項公共建設的重大工程。

examples.en: Justices in the Supreme Court have (life) tenure.
examples.zh: 最高法院大法官有終身任期。

examples.en: She’s a tenured professor at this department.
examples.zh: 她是本系終身聘用的教授。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The rules prohibit dating a coworker.
examples.zh: 規定禁止和同事約會。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Fear can inhibit people from expressing their opinions.
examples.zh: 人們會因為害怕而不敢表達意見。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I’ve decided to move out of the city because of the prohibitive cost of rent.
examples.zh: 因為高昂的房租，讓我決定搬離城市。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This land is government property.
examples.zh: 這塊土地是政府的財產。

examples.en: An important property of gold is that it doesn’t rust.
examples.zh: 黃金有一項重要屬性就是不鏽。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.literal_meaning: to / one’s own / 形容詞字尾
words.definition: [形] 恰當的，合適的 [動] 撥款；挪用

examples.en: What would be an appropriate response to the query?
examples.zh: 這則詢問的恰當回應為何？

examples.en: Congress will appropriate money for the research program.
examples.zh: 國會將撥款資助這項研究計畫。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I lack the proper equipment for the job.
examples.zh: 我缺乏恰當的裝備以進行這項工作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This is proprietary software and cannot be distributed.
examples.zh: 這是有版權的軟體，不可外流。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The new employee is a person of dull apprehension.
examples.zh: 新進員工的理解力很差。

examples.en: The officer was cited for the successful apprehension of the criminal.
examples.zh: 警官受到表揚，因為成功逮捕到罪犯。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 廣泛的，全面的，無所不包的

examples.en: The new driver bought comprehensive auto insurance.
examples.zh: 新駕駛買了汽車全險。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He declined to be named for fear of reprisal.
examples.zh: 他不願提到姓名，因為怕遭到報復。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 使沉澱；存款；存放 [名] 蘊藏；存款

examples.en: Flood waters deposited a thick layer of mud everywhere.
examples.zh: 洪水在四處留下一層厚厚的淤泥。

examples.en: I’m going to the bank to deposit some money.
examples.zh: 我要去銀行存款。

examples.en: There are rich deposits of precious metals in these mountains.
examples.zh: 這片山區有豐富的貴金屬蘊藏。

examples.en: There’s not much money left in my bank deposit.
examples.zh: 我的銀行存款所剩無幾。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The students were told to write a composition on capital punishment.
examples.zh: 學生被告知要寫一篇關於死刑的作文。

examples.en: Critics highly commend the unique composition of the painting.
examples.zh: 評論家高度讚賞這幅畫獨特的構圖。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 建築群；化合物 [形] 複合的 [動] 使加重，使惡化

examples.en: The military compound is fenced in with electric wires.
examples.zh: 軍事營區有電網圍繞。

examples.en: Life on earth is based on organic compounds.
examples.zh: 地球上的生命皆源自有機化合物。

examples.en: The deposit account offers compound interest.
examples.zh: 存款帳戶提供複利。

examples.en: High winds compounded the difficulties of the firefighters.
examples.zh: 強風讓消防隊員面對的困難更為險峻。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 用過即丟的；可自由運用的

examples.en: Disposable diapers are convenient but not environmentally friendly.
examples.zh: 拋棄式紙尿褲很方便但不環保。

examples.en: His disposable income is about half his salary.
examples.zh: 他的可支配所得約佔薪水的一半。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The gangster told his men to dispose of the body.
examples.zh: 歹徒叫手下處理掉屍體。

examples.en: It is very convenient to have a waste disposal in the kitchen.
examples.zh: 廚房裝個廚餘處理器很方便。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You shouldn’t be exposed to direct sunlight around noon.
examples.zh: 中午時段你不該暴露在陽光直射下。

examples.en: This magazine specializes in exposing scandals.
examples.zh: 這本雜誌專門揭發醜聞。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This article is a detailed exposition of Japanese wedding customs.
examples.zh: 這篇文章詳細說明了日本的結婚習俗。

examples.en: The next World Exposition will be held in Paris.
examples.zh: 下一屆世界博覽會將在巴黎舉辦。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: In his retrospective, the artist juxtaposed his early and late works to highlight his progress.
examples.zh: 在回顧展中，藝術家同時展示出早期與晚期的作品，以凸顯他的進步。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Most legislators are in opposition to the tax hike.
examples.zh: 議員大都反對漲稅。

examples.en: The Opposition is loudly condemning the President’s decision to lower tariffs.
examples.zh: 在野黨大力譴責總統調降關稅的決定。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 提出；造成；擺姿勢 [名] 姿勢

examples.en: The depletion of petroleum poses a challenge to us all.
examples.zh: 石油枯竭是對我們所有人的一項挑戰。

examples.en: The reporter asked the interviewee to pose for a cover picture.
examples.zh: 記者請受訪人擺姿勢以拍攝封面照。

examples.en: The interviewee assumed a casual pose.
examples.zh: 受訪人擺出一個自然的姿勢。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 姿勢；姿態 [動] 擺姿勢

examples.en: You should always maintain an erect posture.
examples.zh: 你應該隨時保持挺直的姿勢。

examples.en: The models are posturing for the media.
examples.zh: 模特兒正在擺姿勢供媒體拍照。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The proposal to legalize marijuana has won a lot of support.
examples.zh: 大麻合法化的提案獲得許多人的支持。

examples.en: Did Helen accept John’s proposal?
examples.zh: 海倫有沒有接受約翰的求婚？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: When hyperinflation occurs, everyone suffers.
examples.zh: 發生惡性通貨膨脹時，大家都深受其害。

examples.en: The woman suffers from lung cancer.
examples.zh: 這名婦女罹患肺癌。

examples.en: Two died in the train wreck, and many suffered minor injuries.
examples.zh: 火車事故造成兩人死亡，多人受到輕傷。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The meeting will be held in the conference room.
examples.zh: 會議將在會議室進行。

examples.en: The President will make an important announcement at the press conference.
examples.zh: 總統將在記者會中做出重大宣告。

examples.en: I need to confer with my lawyer.
examples.zh: 我需要和律師商談。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You may choose to defer your admission for one year.
examples.zh: 你可以選擇延後一年入學。

examples.en: The girl deferred to her father’s wishes and stopped seeing the man.
examples.zh: 女孩順從父親的意見，不再和該男子交往。

examples.en: Her deferential attitude pleased her father.
examples.zh: 她順從的態度讓父親很高興。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 渡船；渡口 [動] (用船) 運送；搭船渡河

examples.en: Let’s take the ferry to Kowloon.
examples.zh: 我們搭渡輪到九龍去。

examples.en: There’re many people waiting at the ferry.
examples.zh: 許多人在渡口等船。

examples.en: The boat ferries people and goods all day long.
examples.zh: 這艘船整天在運送客人與貨物。

examples.en: We can ferry across in 10 minutes.
examples.zh: 我們搭船只要10分鐘就可以到達對岸。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 肥沃的；有生育能力的；(想像力) 豐富的

examples.en: The soil is kept fertile by the annual flooding of the river.
examples.zh: 因為河流每年氾濫，土壤得以保持肥沃。

examples.en: Women are fertile before menopause.
examples.zh: 婦女在停經之前都有生育能力。

examples.en: He has a fertile imagination.
examples.zh: 他有豐富的想像力。

examples.en: I need to fertilize the soil, but haven’t decided on the fertilizer.
examples.zh: 我得為土壤施肥，但還沒選定肥料。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I can infer from the article that the author has had first-hand experience in the matter.
examples.zh: 從這篇文章我可以推論，作者有這件事的親身經驗。

examples.en: The party leader, and by inference the whole party, opposed the bill.
examples.zh: 黨主席反對提案，可以推論全黨亦然。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The speaker referred to her wartime experience several times.
examples.zh: 演講人幾度提及她戰時的經驗。

examples.en: The doctor referred the patient to a heart specialist.
examples.zh: 醫生將病人轉介給一位心臟科專家。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The referee blew the whistle and nullified the goal.
examples.zh: 裁判吹哨，判定得分無效。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I can find no reference of the new theory in this paper.
examples.zh: 我在這篇論文中沒看到提及新理論。

examples.en: You can find the book in the reference room at the library.
examples.zh: 你可以在圖書館參考室找到這本書。

examples.en: You need to provide three references for your application.
examples.zh: 你的申請需要提出三封推薦信。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: After a year the student transferred to another department.
examples.zh: 一年後，該名學生轉到其他系。

examples.en: The internet greatly facilitates the transfer of information.
examples.zh: 網路大幅便利資訊的傳遞。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: A “boom box” is a large portable stereo player.
examples.zh: 所謂 boom box 指的是大型手提式立體音響。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This trading company imports hi-tech gadgets and exports raw material.
examples.zh: 這家貿易公司進口高科技產品，並出口原料。

examples.en: It is in the import-export business.
examples.zh: 那屬於進出口行業。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I want to take a photo at the portal of the Lincoln Memorial.
examples.zh: 我要到林肯紀念堂入口拍照。

examples.en: What is your favorite internet portal?
examples.zh: 你最喜歡的入口網站是哪一個？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Good public transport helps reduce pollution and traffic congestion.
examples.zh: 良好的公共運輸可以降低汙染、減少交通阻塞。

examples.en: Pipelines and tankers are used to transport petroleum.
examples.zh: 油管與油輪用來運送石油。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Traffic congestion is a real problem for commuters.
examples.zh: 交通阻塞對通勤族來說是個大問題。

examples.en: The main roads are badly congested during rush hour.
examples.zh: 主要道路在尖峰時刻嚴重塞車。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Stomach acid helps digest food.
examples.zh: 胃酸幫助消化食物。

examples.en: I need some time to digest the lecture.
examples.zh: 我需要一點時間來消化這場演講。

examples.en: The institute publishes a monthly digest of its research findings.
examples.zh: 該機構每月出版一份文摘，介紹研究結果。

examples.en: You may have indigestion if you eat too much.
examples.zh: 吃太多你會消化不良。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 姿勢；表態 [動] 打手勢

examples.en: The man shrugged and opened his hands, in a gesture of helplessness.
examples.zh: 此人聳聳肩、兩手一攤，擺出一付無奈的姿勢。

examples.en: The small donation was a gesture of humanitarian concern.
examples.zh: 小額捐款是人道關懷的表態。

examples.en: He gestured for me to follow.
examples.zh: 他打個手勢叫我跟上。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Liquid food is easy to ingest.
examples.zh: 流質食物很容易吞嚥。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The winner of the lottery contributed large sums to charity.
examples.zh: 樂透得主捐出鉅款給慈善機關。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 特性，特質 [動] 將…歸因於

examples.en: Both candidates possess the attributes we want in a leader.
examples.zh: 兩名候選人都具有我們想要的領袖人物特質。

examples.en: The media attributed the success of the expedition entirely to one man.
examples.zh: 媒體把探險隊的成功完全歸功於一人。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Donated foodstuffs were quickly distributed to the stricken areas.
examples.zh: 捐贈的食品很快就被分發到災區。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The killer acted without fear of retribution.
examples.zh: 這名殺手行動時完全不怕遭到報應。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 進貢國；支流 [形] 進貢的；從屬的

examples.en: This stream is a tributary of the Ohio River.
examples.zh: 這條溪是俄亥俄河的支流。

examples.en: The Han Dynasty lorded over many tributary nations.
examples.zh: 漢朝統治許多藩屬國。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: In the ceremony, artists and musicians paid tribute to the famous composer.
examples.zh: 在典禮中，藝術家與音樂家都對該大作曲家致敬。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: On an impulse, I asked the girl her phone number.
examples.zh: 一股衝動之下，我向女孩要了電話號碼。

examples.en: Items such as chewing gum and batteries are placed near checkout counters to attract impulse buying.
examples.zh: 口香糖、電池等品項被放在結帳櫃檯附近，以吸引衝動型消費。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The boat is propelled by a 30-horsepower engine.
examples.zh: 這艘船是由一個具有 30 馬力的引擎所推動。

examples.en: The hit song propelled the singer to stardom.
examples.zh: 這首熱門歌曲將該歌手推向大明星地位。

examples.en: A propeller plane flies much slower than a jet plane.
examples.zh: 螺旋槳飛機的速度比噴射機慢得多。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Do we have enough men to repel enemy attacks?
examples.zh: 我們是否有足夠人力擊退敵人的攻擊？

examples.en: Similar poles of magnets repel each other.
examples.zh: 磁力同性相斥。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 令人厭惡的；防水的 [名] 防水劑；驅蟲劑

examples.en: I find such cruelty utterly repellent.
examples.zh: 我對這種殘酷行為深感厭惡。

examples.en: This jacket is water-repellent.
examples.zh: 這件外套防水。

examples.en: Spray some insect repellent on yourself.
examples.zh: 噴點防蟲劑在身上。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: At the repulsive sight, she averted her eyes.
examples.zh: 對此令人厭惡的景象，她移開目光。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 抽出，拔出 [名] 精（油）；選文

examples.en: Oil is extracted from underground reserves.
examples.zh: 從地下蘊藏處抽出石油。

examples.en: The dentist decided to extract the tooth.
examples.zh: 牙醫決定要拔牙。

examples.en: The shampoo has herbal extracts added.
examples.zh: 洗髮精頭添加了植物精油。

examples.en: This passage is an extract from the new bestseller.
examples.zh: 這段文字摘自那本新的暢銷書。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The magician distracted his audience with a lot of sound and smoke.
examples.zh: 魔術師用許多音效與煙霧分散觀眾的注意力。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: There’s no need to protract the discussion; we can make a decision right now.
examples.zh: 不必再延長討論了，我們可以現在就做決定。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Calculate profit by subtracting cost from revenue.
examples.zh: 利潤的算法是營收扣掉成本。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: If we work together, we can surmount the obstacle.
examples.zh: 如果我們合作，就可以克服這個障礙。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: Many eminent surgeons are on the hospital’s staff.
examples.zh: 許多著名的外科醫生都在這家醫院工作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: There may be difficulties in the long run, but there’s no imminent danger.
examples.zh: 長遠來看或許有困難，但並沒有立即的危險。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: Keeping the exhibit safe is a matter of paramount importance.
examples.zh: 維護展品的安全，這是最重要的事。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

words.definition: [形] 突出的，顯著的；著名的

examples.en: He placed the award in a prominent position on his desk.
examples.zh: 他把獎牌放在桌上一個顯著的位置。

examples.en: The professor is a prominent scholar.
examples.zh: 這名教授是位著名的學者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: The lighthouse stood on the windswept promontory overlooking the bay.
examples.zh: 燈塔立在風大的海角，俯視海灣。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: They slowly ascended the steep path up to the mountain.
examples.zh: 他們沿著陡峭的小路緩緩爬上山。

examples.en: There’s a long flight of steps ascending to the cathedral doors.
examples.zh: 有一長段階梯上通往大教堂門口。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

words.definition: [動] 屈尊，以恩賜態度對待

examples.en: I wonder if your boss will condescend to visit us?
examples.zh: 不知道你老闆會不會賞臉來拜訪我們？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

examples.en: The path descended steeply into the valley.
examples.zh: 小路陡降到山谷中。

examples.en: She slowly descended the stairs.
examples.zh: 她慢慢走下樓梯。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「突出」與「攀爬」的字根

words.definition: [名] 行為 [動] 領導；指揮；表現

examples.en: The club has a strict code of conduct.
examples.zh: 社團有嚴格的行為守則。

examples.en: The experiment was conducted by scientists in New York.
examples.zh: 實驗是由紐約的科學家主持的。

examples.en: The orchestra was conducted by a great maestro.
examples.zh: 管弦樂團由一位大師指揮。

examples.en: I hardly know how to conduct myself in the presence of the King.
examples.zh: 在國王面前我簡直是手足無措。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The woman was abducted at gunpoint.
examples.zh: 這名婦女被持槍挾持。

examples.en: The police quickly cracked the abduction case and rescued the missing children.
examples.zh: 警方迅速偵破綁架案，救出失蹤的兒童。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: This pipeline is the main conduit for Alaskan oil.
examples.zh: 這條油管是阿拉斯加石油主要的導管。

examples.en: The internet has become an important conduit of information.
examples.zh: 網路成為重要的資訊管道。

examples.en: The strait is a conduit for merchant ships.
examples.zh: 海峽是商船的通道。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: From these premises, one conclusion can be deduced.
examples.zh: 從這幾項前提，可以推斷出一項結論。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The player had points deducted for arguing with the referee.
examples.zh: 球員因為和裁判發生爭執，遭到扣分。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The characteristic ductility of copper makes it highly versatile.
examples.zh: 銅的延展性特性使它用途甚廣。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: From these observations, we can induce that most mammals are equipped with hair.
examples.zh: 根據這些觀察，我們可以歸納出大部分的哺乳類動物都有毛髮。

examples.en: You can induce vomit by sticking a finger down the throat.
examples.zh: 你只要把手指頭向喉嚨就可以催吐。

examples.en: Francis Bacon advocated induction and deduction.
examples.zh: 法蘭西斯·培根提倡歸納法和演繹法。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

words.definition: [動] 使就職；接受入會；徵召（入伍）

examples.en: She was inducted as president of the college.
examples.zh: 她就任院長。

examples.en: The club will induct six new members this year.
examples.zh: 社團今年將接受六名新會員入會。

examples.en: Michael Jordan was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame.
examples.zh: 麥可·喬丹正式入選籃球名人堂。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The factory invited a consultant over to study ways to raise productivity.
examples.zh: 工廠請來顧問，以研究辦法提高生產力。

examples.en: The investigation produced quick results.
examples.zh: 調查很快就有了結果。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: Warning signs were installed along the curve to reduce accidents.
examples.zh: 沿著彎道設置警告號誌以減少車禍。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The official succumbed to seduction and stole classified information for the woman.
examples.zh: 官員屈服於誘惑，為這名女子竊取機密情報。

examples.en: He was seduced by the woman.
examples.zh: 他受到這名女子的誘惑。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: Let’s wait to see the subsequent developments.
examples.zh: 我們等著看後續的發展。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The Chairman served for three consecutive terms.
examples.zh: 主席連續三年連任。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: You have more money; consequently, you should pay.
examples.zh: 你比較有錢，所以你該付錢。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The relocation was executed according to plan.
examples.zh: 搬遷工作按照計畫執行。

examples.en: The criminal will be executed early tomorrow morning.
examples.zh: 犯人將於明日清晨被處決。

examples.en: The execution of the plan went on schedule.
examples.zh: 計畫的執行按照時間表。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

words.definition: [名] 高階主管；行政主管 / [形] 執行的，管理的；行政的

examples.en: She is now a senior executive, having worked her way up through the company.
examples.zh: 她從基層一路往上爬，如今已是公司的高階主管。

examples.en: His executive skills are what can save the company now.
examples.zh: 現在只有靠他的經營管理能力才能挽救公司了。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

words.literal_meaning: not / together / follow / 形容詞字尾
words.definition: [形] 不重要的，無足輕重的

examples.en: What I think is inconsequential; it’s what you feel about the matter that counts.
examples.zh: 我怎麼想不重要，重要的是你對此事的看法。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: The police pursued the criminal across the country.
examples.zh: 警察在全國追捕這名犯人。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: Have you seen the latest sequel to the Bond movies?
examples.zh: 007電影的最新一集你看了嗎？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「引導」與「跟隨」的字根

examples.en: If you persist, eventually you’ll succeed.
examples.zh: 你只要堅持到底，遲早會成功的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: With the advent of the Industrial Revolution, manufacturing was revolutionized.
examples.zh: 隨著工業革命的到來，生產製造業掀起革命。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Boutiques line both sides of the avenue.
examples.zh: 大道兩旁都是精品店。

examples.en: Satellites opened up new avenues of communication.
examples.zh: 衛星開啟了通訊的新管道。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The saying “Do in Rome as the Romans do” means you should follow convention.
examples.zh: 「在羅馬，照著羅馬人做」這句話諺語的意思是要你入境隨俗。

examples.en: Our annual conference is at the convention center.
examples.zh: 我們的年會在會議中心舉行。

examples.en: The Presidential candidate is officially nominated in the quadrennial Republican Convention.
examples.zh: 總統候選人在四年一度的共和黨全國代表大會中正式提名。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The retail chain doubled revenues this year by opening 20 new shops.
examples.zh: 這家零售連鎖店今年開設20家新門市，營收因此翻倍。

examples.en: The Internal Revenue Service is responsible for tax collection.
examples.zh: 國稅局負責收稅。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 冒險 / [名] (有風險的)企業；商業投機

examples.en: The explorers ventured into the Amazon jungle.
examples.zh: 探險家冒險深入亞馬遜森林。

examples.en: If you want to start a business there, you must form a joint venture with a local firm.
examples.zh: 想在那裡開辦企業，你得找一家當地公司一起合資。

examples.en: The man persuaded the venture capitalist and got the capital he needed.
examples.zh: 此人說服創投業者，取得他需要的資金。

examples.en: Our hike in the mountains was quite an adventure.
examples.zh: 我們在山上的健行相當驚險刺激。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You can find a detailed itinerary of the tour in this brochure.
examples.zh: 這份簡介中有這趟旅遊的詳細行程。

examples.en: There were many itinerant preachers in colonial times.
examples.zh: 殖民地時代有許多巡迴講道的牧師。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 起初的 / [名] 首字母縮寫

examples.en: The man’s initial reaction to the news was one of surprise.
examples.zh: 聽到該消息，此人最初的反應是大吃一驚。

examples.en: Your initials—J.F.K.—are just like Kennedy’s.
examples.zh: 你的姓名首字母縮寫 J.F.K. 和甘迺迪的一樣。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 開始；使了解；接納(新成員) / [名] 新加入者

examples.en: The Chairman initiated a program of reform.
examples.zh: 主席啟動了改革計畫。

examples.en: At 10, he was initiated into the art of golf by his father.
examples.zh: 他十歲時就在父親的引導下進入高爾夫球的世界。

examples.en: New members are initiated into the fraternity in a riotous ceremony.
examples.zh: 大學兄弟會有一套喧鬧的儀式迎接新會員入會。

examples.en: These secrets of the organization are known only to a small group of initiates.
examples.zh: 組織的這些祕密只有一部分新成員知道。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 倡議；主動性；主動的行動

examples.en: The governor has proposed a new initiative to improve conditions in urban schools.
examples.zh: 州長提出新倡議，要改善都會區學校的情況。

examples.en: This worker is responsible but lacks initiative.
examples.zh: 這名員工做事負責，但缺乏主動性。

examples.en: If you want to know her, you must take the initiative and introduce yourself.
examples.zh: 你想認識她的話，得採取行動去自我介紹。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 短暫的；路過的 / [名] 過往旅客；候鳥

examples.en: Many transient laborers work in these farms in summer.
examples.zh: 夏天有許多短暫工在這些農場打工。

examples.en: Hotels come in three kinds: transient, resort, and residential.
examples.zh: 旅館分成三種：短期住宿、度假區型以及長住型。

examples.en: Swallows are transients; thrushes are residents.
examples.zh: 燕子是候鳥，畫眉是留鳥。

examples.en: He turned to religion when he realized the transitory nature of fame and wealth.
examples.zh: 當他明白名與利的本質都不長久，就轉而投入宗教的世界了。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: We’ve completed the transition from a labor-intensive to a hi-tech form of economy.
examples.zh: 我們的經濟轉型已經完全從勞力密集轉型到高科技產業。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Proceed to the next window when you have finished here.
examples.zh: 這裡辦完後請前進到下個窗口。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 配件；共犯 / [形] 附屬的

examples.en: This designer handbag is an excellent fashion accessory.
examples.zh: 這款名家設計的包包是絕佳的時尚配件。

examples.en: The murderer’s confidant was charged as an accessory to the crime.
examples.zh: 凶手的密友以共犯名被起訴。

examples.en: The investigation revealed accessory details, but did not affect the conclusion.
examples.zh: 調查透露了相關的細節，但沒有影響到結果。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: On election night, the loser conceded defeat in a televised speech.
examples.zh: 大選之夜，輸的一方在電視上發表演說、承認失敗。

examples.en: I concede the point, but your argument is flawed in other ways.
examples.zh: 我承認你講的這點沒錯，但你的論證還有其他瑕疵。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 承認；讓步；特許(銷售)權

examples.en: The negotiator is prepared to make some small concessions to get the deal done.
examples.zh: 協商者打算透過一些小讓步以達成交易。

examples.en: Before the movie started, they went to the concession stand to get some popcorn.
examples.zh: 電影開演前，他們到販賣部去買爆米花。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Capital punishment would be excessive for this case.
examples.zh: 這個案子判死刑就超過了。

examples.en: You should be careful not to eat to excess, with your weak stomach.
examples.zh: 你腸胃不好，小心別吃過量。

examples.en: I have to do something about my excess weight.
examples.zh: 我的體重過重，得想想辦法。

examples.en: The camera will be triggered if you exceed the speed limit.
examples.zh: 如果超速，攝影機就會啟動。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 無法取得的；無路可通的

derivatives.meaning: [名] 接近；通道 / [動] 存取

examples.en: The classified information is inaccessible to the general public.
examples.zh: 一般民眾無法取得機密情報。

examples.en: The projected blacktop will provide easy access to this remote scenic spot.
examples.zh: 計畫中的柏油路將為這處偏遠的景點提供方便的通道。

examples.en: You cannot access the data unless you have the password.
examples.zh: 除非你有密碼，否則無法取得資料。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The archbishop interceded on behalf of the condemned prisoner and obtained a pardon for him.
examples.zh: 大主教為囚犯調停，取得了特赦。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The new CEO outdid his predecessor by doubling revenues in his first year.
examples.zh: 新任執行長的表現超過前任，第一年就讓營收翻了一倍。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: What is your standard operating procedure in the event of a hijacking?
examples.zh: 遇上劫持時，你們的標準作業程序為何？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 過程，進程 / [動] 處理

examples.en: The process of evolution never ceases.
examples.zh: 演化的過程永不休止。

examples.en: This mainframe computer processes information at lightning speed.
examples.zh: 這台電腦主機處理資訊快如閃電。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: After the typhoon, the flood waters gradually receded.
examples.zh: 颱風過後，洪水逐漸消退。

examples.en: His receding hairline indicates his age.
examples.zh: 他後退的髮際線透露出他的年齡。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 休會；下課時間；(牆壁等的)凹處

examples.en: The judge called a recess.
examples.zh: 法官宣告休會。

examples.en: The children are playing at recess.
examples.zh: 小孩在下課時間玩耍。

examples.en: There is a pot of plants in the recess.
examples.zh: 壁凹處擺了一盆植物。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Our team has won the championship for the fifth successive year.
examples.zh: 我們的隊伍連續第五年贏得冠軍。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I have the author's permission to quote from his article.
examples.zh: 我有作者的許可，可以引述他的文章。

examples.en: Smoking is not permitted in this restaurant.
examples.zh: 這家餐廳禁止吸菸。

examples.en: A foreigner needs a work permit to work here.
examples.zh: 外國人需要許可證才能在此工作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The young man remits half his monthly salary to his parents back home.
examples.zh: 這名年輕人把一半月薪匯回去給父母。

examples.en: The governor remitted the remainder of the woman's life sentence.
examples.zh: 政府豁免了該女子無期徒刑的剩餘刑期。

examples.en: The cancer is currently in remission.
examples.zh: 癌症目前在緩解期。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You must submit your application package before the deadline.
examples.zh: 你得在截止日期前提交所有的申請資料。

examples.en: The witness submitted to pressure and refused to testify.
examples.zh: 證人屈服於壓力，拒絕出庭作證。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The message is transmitted over short-wave radio.
examples.zh: 訊息是以短波無線電傳送的。

examples.en: Malaria is transmitted by mosquitoes.
examples.zh: 瘧疾是由蚊子所傳播。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I’m calling to inquire about your new product.
examples.zh: 我打電話來是想詢問你們的新產品。

examples.en: Please inquire after your father for me.
examples.zh: 請代我問候令尊。

examples.en: The committee is inquiring into the cause of the disaster.
examples.zh: 委員會正在調查災難的起因。

examples.en: The trading company sends out dozens of letters of inquiry a month.
examples.zh: 貿易公司每月發出幾十封業務開發信。

examples.en: The inquisitive child is always asking “why.”
examples.zh: 好奇的小孩老是要問「為什麼」。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The MBA student is interested in mergers and acquisitions.
examples.zh: 這名企管碩士的興趣在於併購。

examples.en: The museum’s latest acquisitions cost a fortune.
examples.zh: 博物館最近購入的收藏花了不少錢。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Alexander the Great conquered most of the Western world.
examples.zh: 亞歷山大大帝征服了大部分的西方世界。

examples.en: Alexander was a great conqueror.
examples.zh: 亞歷山大是一位偉大的征服者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I bought an exquisite crystal figurine at the gift shop.
examples.zh: 我在禮品店買了一個精美的小水晶雕像。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.literal_meaning: before / again / ask / 形容詞字尾
words.definition: [形] 不可或缺的 [名] 先決條件，必備條件

examples.en: Does the candidate have all the prerequisite skills?
examples.zh: 這名求職者是否具備所有必要的技能？

examples.en: To study in the U.S., a sufficient command of the English language is a prerequisite.
examples.zh: 要到美國讀書，對英語有充分的掌握是先決條件。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The young man went to the big city in quest of fame and wealth.
examples.zh: 年輕人到大城市去追名逐利。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: He is a docile pupil.
examples.zh: 他是個聽話的學生。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Those fundamentalists have a culture of dogmatism and fanaticism.
examples.zh: 那些基本教義派人士的文化就是教條主義與狂熱偏執。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: I was surprised by the orthodoxy of her political views.
examples.zh: 沒想到她的政治觀點如此正統。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] (背後可能有道理的) 矛盾

examples.en: There’s a simple explanation behind the apparent paradox of simultaneous unemployment and skilled-labor shortage.
examples.zh: 失業問題和有技能的勞工短缺同時並存，表面看來矛盾，不過背後有個簡單的理由。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Those disputatious academicians are always arguing.
examples.zh: 那些愛爭論的學者老是爭論不休。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 無庸置疑的，無可爭辯的

examples.en: There is indisputable proof that the suspect is guilty.
examples.zh: 不容置疑的證據證明嫌犯有罪。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The defendant repudiated all the allegations.
examples.zh: 被告否認所有指控。

examples.en: The UN resolution repudiated aggression and violence.
examples.zh: 聯合國決議案排斥侵略與暴力。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 名氣，名聲 [動] 相信，認為是

examples.en: I know a repair shop of good repute.
examples.zh: 我知道有家名聲不錯的修理店。

examples.en: The chef is reputed to be the best in these parts.
examples.zh: 一般認為這位大廚在這幾個部分是最出色的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: That plan has the approbation of the school board.
examples.zh: 該計畫獲得學校董事的批准。

examples.en: Their hard work deserves approbation.
examples.zh: 他們工作辛勞，值得嘉獎。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The team made an improbable comeback.
examples.zh: 這個隊伍起死回生，令人感到不可思議。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The judge is a person of indisputable probity.
examples.zh: 這位法官的正直無庸置疑。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 指責，斥責 [形] 道德淪喪的 [名] 道德淪喪者

examples.en: The columnist reprobated the laxity of the age.
examples.zh: 專欄作家斥責這時代的綱紀不彰。

examples.en: He is a reprobate judge who could be bribed.
examples.zh: 他是名可以被賄賂的墮落法官。

examples.en: The government started a program for rehabilitating hard-core reprobates.
examples.zh: 政府啟動計畫，要改造十惡不赦的道德淪喪者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The winner in the primaries will be nominated as the party’s Presidential candidate.
examples.zh: 初選獲勝者將被提名為政黨的總統候選人。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The investigation ended in the chairman’s ignominious abdication.
examples.zh: 調查案以主席頹頭下台告終。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 誤植人名；不恰當的名稱

examples.en: “Grand Hotel” is a complete misnomer for that cheap place I stayed.
examples.zh: 我住的那個廉價場所取名「大飯店」，是真正的名不符實。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 名義上的；微不足道的，象徵性的

examples.en: The British monarch serves as nominal head of state.
examples.zh: 英國君主是個虛位元首。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Many authors like to use pseudonyms.
examples.zh: 許多作家喜歡用假名。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The priest absolved the poor sinner.
examples.zh: 神父赦免了可憐的罪人。

examples.en: The result of the investigation absolved the accused of all charges.
examples.zh: 調查結果免除了被告所有的罪名。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 溶解；解除（契約等）；解散

examples.en: Sugar dissolves in hot water faster than in cold water.
examples.zh: 糖在熱水中溶解的速度比在冷水中快。

examples.en: The treatment is used for the dissolution of kidney stones.
examples.zh: 這種療法用於溶解腎結石。

examples.en: The dissolution of the marriage dragged on for months.
examples.zh: 婚姻的解除拖了好幾個月。

examples.en: Vitamin C is water-soluble.
examples.zh: 維生素C可溶於水。

examples.en: The sugar solution tastes sweet.
examples.zh: 糖水是甜的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 解決（方法）；決心；解析度

examples.en: We found a resolution to the dispute.
examples.zh: 這場爭議，我們找到了解決辦法。

examples.en: What are your New Year’s resolutions?
examples.zh: 你的新年新希望是什麼？

examples.en: This expensive monitor has very high resolutions.
examples.zh: 這款高價位螢幕的解析度很高。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 解決；決定 [名] 決心

examples.en: The brothers finally resolved their conflict.
examples.zh: 兩兄弟終於解了衝突。

examples.en: The woman resolved to lose weight by any means possible.
examples.zh: 該女子決心不擇手段減重。

examples.en: She showed great resolve for the first three days.
examples.zh: 頭三天她意志堅定。

examples.en: He has remained resolute in his opposition to the bill.
examples.zh: 他反對提案的立場一直很堅定。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The clever man solved the problem in five seconds.
examples.zh: 這個聰明的人只花五秒就解決了問題。

examples.en: Have you found a solution to the problem?
examples.zh: 你找到解決問題的方法了嗎？

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [形] 有償債能力的 [名] 溶劑

examples.en: The company stayed solvent by selling off a branch.
examples.zh: 公司賣掉一部分以維持償債能力。

examples.en: You need an industrial solvent for the paint.
examples.zh: 你需要工業溶劑來調製油漆。

examples.en: Faced with insolvency, the company had to layoff some workers.
examples.zh: 公司因無力償還岌岌可危，只得遣散一些員工。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The university has been lax about enforcing these rules.
examples.zh: 大學在執行這些規定上一直都很鬆懈。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: It was a hot, languid summer day.
examples.zh: 這是個炎熱、慵懶的夏日。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Symptoms of the disease include paleness and lassitude.
examples.zh: 這種病的病徵包括臉色蒼白、全身乏力。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: This lemonade will slake your thirst.
examples.zh: 這杯檸檬水可以解渴。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 緊張，壓力 [動] 繃緊，用力

examples.en: The new manager is under a lot of strain.
examples.zh: 新任經理面對很大的壓力。

examples.en: She strained her eyes to see better in the gloom.
examples.zh: 她在昏暗的燈光下眼睛用力想看清楚。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [名] 沮喪，苦惱 [動] 使苦惱

examples.en: The student showed signs of distress after he failed in the important exam.
examples.zh: 學生在重要的考試中失利，表現出沮喪的徵兆。

examples.en: The bad news distressed everybody.
examples.zh: 壞消息讓所有人都很沮喪。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: You should restrain yourself at those all-you-can-eat restaurants.
examples.zh: 去吃到飽餐廳用餐時，你要有所節制。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Under the curfew, freedom of movement in and out of the city is restricted.
examples.zh: 因為宵禁，進出城市的自由受到了限制。

examples.en: The government has placed new restrictions on the sale of alcohol.
examples.zh: 針對酒類的販售，政府添加了新的規定。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The postmortem reveals that the woman was strangled to death.
examples.zh: 驗屍結果顯示該女子是被勒死的。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Ms. Lin is a strict teacher who made a lot of stringent rules.
examples.zh: 林女士是位嚴格的老師，訂了許多嚴格的規定。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Some insects make use of surface tension and walk on water.
examples.zh: 有些昆蟲利用表面張力在水上行走。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

words.definition: [動] 減弱，降低 [形] 減弱的

examples.en: Earplugs will attenuate the loud sounds of the machinery.
examples.zh: 耳塞可以降低機器的噪音。

examples.en: Under the assault of science, the doctrines of Christianity became attenuate and distorted.
examples.zh: 在科學的攻擊下，基督教的教條變得非常脆弱並遭到扭曲。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Without an extension, the contract will be terminated at the end of the year.
examples.zh: 若不展延，合約將在年底終止。

examples.en: The beach extends for over two kilometers.
examples.zh: 海灘綿延超過兩公里。

examples.en: After an extensive search, the police managed to locate most of the victims.
examples.zh: 經過大規模的搜索，警方成功找到了大部分的受害者。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: The injury caused her to feel intense pain.
examples.zh: 受傷讓她感到劇烈的疼痛。

examples.en: You can feel intense heat coming from the furnace.
examples.zh: 你可以感覺到從火爐傳來的高溫。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類

examples.en: Who tends the garden for you?
examples.zh: 誰幫你照料花園？

examples.en: Old people tend to be forgetful.
examples.zh: 老人有健忘的傾向。

examples.en: The man has a tendency to exaggerate.
examples.zh: 此人有誇大的傾向。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

words.definition: [形] 溫柔的 [名] 投標 [動] 提出

examples.en: The tough man turns out to have a tender heart.
examples.zh: 這名強悍的男子卻有個溫柔的內心。

examples.en: The company’s tender for the building contract has been accepted.
examples.zh: 公司爭取建築合約的投標已經被接受了。

examples.en: After the sex scandal, the man tendered his resignation.
examples.zh: 性醜聞爆發之後，此人遞出了辭呈。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The tenuous rope is fully capable of supporting my weight.
examples.zh: 這條細繩可以完全支撐我的體重。

examples.en: You can easily exhaust yourself in the high mountains because of the tenuous air.
examples.zh: 因為空氣稀薄，在高山上很容易疲累。

examples.en: His tenuous reasoning is not convincing at all.
examples.zh: 他薄弱的推理毫無說服力。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: Fragments of the broken vase covered the floor.
examples.zh: 破花瓶的碎片撒了一地。

examples.en: The vase fragmented into a thousand pieces.
examples.zh: 花瓶碎成了一千片。

examples.en: Several witnesses gave fragmentary accounts of what had actually happened.
examples.zh: 幾名證人對於到底發生了什麼事，提出了片段的說法。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The colors of the rainbow are the result of water droplets diffracting light.
examples.zh: 彩虹的顏色來自於小水珠造成的衍射。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The value of pi is an infinite fraction.
examples.zh: 圓周率的值是個無限小數。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

words.definition: [名] 破裂，斷裂 [動] (使) 破裂，(使) 斷裂

examples.en: The man suffered multiple bone fractures in the accident.
examples.zh: 該場意外導致此人身體多處骨折。

examples.en: He fractured several ribs.
examples.zh: 他摔斷了好幾根肋骨。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The sign says “Fragile. Handle with care.”
examples.zh: 標籤上寫著「易碎物品，小心搬運」。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The patient is still too frail to walk.
examples.zh: 病人還太虛弱，不能走動。

examples.en: “Frailty, thy name is Woman!”
examples.zh: 「弱者，你的名字是女人！」

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: This product clearly infringes a patent held by our company.
examples.zh: 這項產品很明顯侵犯到本公司的專利。

examples.en: The curfew infringes upon the rights of private citizens.
examples.zh: 宵禁侵犯到一般公民的權利。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: The negotiation was disrupted when protestors started throwing eggs.
examples.zh: 抗議人士開始扔雞蛋，談判於是破裂。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: You should avoid any abrupt movements on the little boat.
examples.zh: 在小船上要避免任何突然的動作。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

derivatives.meaning: [形] 破產的 [動] 使破產

examples.en: The company went from insolvency to bankruptcy.
examples.zh: 公司從無償債能力到宣告破產。

examples.en: It was bankrupted by a misguided investment.
examples.zh: 因為一項不當投資而導致破產。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

derivatives.meaning: [形] 貪汙的 [動] 貪汙；敗壞

examples.en: The biggest problem now is widespread corruption among government officials.
examples.zh: 目前最大的問題就是政府官員普遍貪汙。

examples.en: Even the police are corrupt.
examples.zh: 連警察都貪汙。

examples.en: You shouldn’t corrupt young people with your decadent philosophy.
examples.zh: 別用你那套頹廢的哲學帶壞年輕人。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: Violence erupted when the pro-life and pro-choice crowds came to the same square.
examples.zh: 反墮胎與支持墮胎的兩隊人馬來到同一座廣場，於是爆發了暴力衝突。

examples.en: The volcanic eruption went on intermittently for days.
examples.zh: 火山爆發間斷了好多天。

words.part_name: Part 2 通用學術字彙：依字根分類
words.chapter_name: 表示「破壞」與「建造」的字根

examples.en: Construction ahead. Please detour.
examples.zh: 前方施工，請改道。
